assertions.title: Authenticated requests receive a Bedrock or Z.AI response through the gateway

steps.observed: Page loaded successfully with auth entry UI (Register/Sign In buttons visible)

steps.action: Set up virtual WebAuthn authenticator via Playwright CDP
steps.expected: Virtual authenticator ready for passkey registration

steps.action: Register new user with passkey via /auth/register/begin + /auth/register/finish
steps.observed: Registration successful - user gateway-e2e-1775949526815-d340om created and session cookies set (choir_access, choir_refresh)

steps.expected: Session shows authenticated: true with user identity
steps.observed: Session confirmed authenticated - cookies transferred to agent-browser session

steps.action: Reload to reach authenticated shell with prompt input
steps.expected: Shell UI loads with task runner and prompt input visible
steps.observed: Shell UI loaded successfully - shows 'Ask the runtime...' input, Send button, Sign Out button, user identity displayed

steps.action: Submit runtime prompt through proxy to gateway to Z.AI provider
steps.expected: Task submission returns stable handle, flows through gateway to Z.AI provider, response appears in UI
steps.observed: Task submission failed - Bootstrap failed (502), Task submission failed (502). Proxy cannot connect to sandbox WebSocket at 127.0.0.1:9001

evidence.consoleErrors: Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 502 () - multiple occurrences on bootstrap and task submission
proxyLogs: proxy WS: dial sandbox ws://127.0.0.1:9001/api/ws: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:9001: connect: no route to host
http: proxy error: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:9001: connect: no route to host

assertions.issues: End-to-end flow blocked by proxy/sandbox routing mismatch. The proxy is configured for vmctl_routing and attempts to connect to sandbox at 127.0.0.1:9001 (per-user sandbox port), but the actual sandbox process is listening on 127.0.0.1:8085. No VM ownership file exists at /var/lib/go-choir/vmctl/ownership.json, indicating per-user VM routing is not fully operational despite vmctl_routing being 'enabled'.

frictions.description: WebAuthn passkey registration requires Playwright with virtual authenticator - standard agent-browser cannot complete WebAuthn ceremony
frictions.resolution: Used Playwright with CDP virtual authenticator (WebAuthn.addVirtualAuthenticator) to automate passkey registration, then transferred cookies to agent-browser

frictions.description: Agent-browser session cookie transfer required manual cookie setting - playwright state file not automatically shared
frictions.resolution: Manually extracted cookies from Playwright and set them via agent-browser cookies set command

frictions.description: Proxy routing configuration mismatch - proxy dials 127.0.0.1:9001 but sandbox listens on 127.0.0.1:8085

blockers.description: Proxy cannot establish WebSocket connection to sandbox. Proxy is configured to dial ws://127.0.0.1:9001/api/ws for per-user sandbox routing (vmctl_routing: enabled), but sandbox process is listening on port 8085. No VM ownership file exists at /var/lib/go-choir/vmctl/ownership.json. The vmctl routing appears enabled but per-user sandbox instances are not being created or are on different ports than expected.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified all services healthy via /health endpoints. Sandbox confirmed ready on port 8085 with active_provider: gateway. Proxy confirmed healthy with vmctl_routing: enabled. SSH inspection shows 'no route to host' errors when proxy attempts to connect to port 9001.

summary: Tested VAL-GATEWAY-001 end-to-end flow. Authentication layer fully functional - WebAuthn passkey registration, session management, and shell bootstrap UI all working correctly. Gateway service healthy with Z.AI provider configured (3 active identities). However, runtime task submission blocked by proxy/sandbox routing mismatch. Proxy expects per-user sandbox at 127.0.0.1:9001 but sandbox listens on 8085; vmctl routing appears enabled but per-user VM instances not operational. Status: BLOCKED (infrastructure routing issue prevents end-to-end provider flow).
